Implements a version display window for manufacturing function tests with button interaction handling.

# Purpose
The code is a C header file that defines functionality for a version display window in a manufacturing function test application. It includes several header files that provide necessary dependencies, such as backlight control, button handling, font management, graphics utilities, resource management, logging, and version control. The file defines a `VersionData` structure to hold application-specific data and a text layer for displaying version information.

The code implements functions to manage the lifecycle of a version window, including loading and unloading the window, configuring button click handlers, and retrieving version information. The [`version_get_version_str`](<#version_get_version_str>) function collects firmware metadata, bootloader version, and system resource version, formatting them into a string for display. The [`push_version_window`](<#push_version_window>) function initializes and displays the version window, setting up necessary handlers and configurations. The window responds to button events, specifically waiting for two button-up events before closing, to handle propagation from the quality control application launch.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/backlight.h`
- `drivers/button.h`
- `applib/fonts/fonts.h`
- `git_version.auto.h`
- `applib/graphics/utf8.h`
- `resource/resource.h`
- `resource/resource_ids.auto.h`
- `system/bootbits.h`
- `system/logging.h`
- `applib/ui/ui.h`
- `applib/ui/window_private.h`
- `system/version.h`
- `stdlib.h`
- `stdio.h`
- `inttypes.h`


# Global Variables

---
### s\_click\_count
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: A static integer variable that counts the number of button-up events in the version window.
- **Use**: Used to track the number of button-up events to determine when to close the version window.


---
### s\_version\_str
- **Type**: ``char*``
- **Description**: A pointer to a character array that stores the version string of the firmware. It is dynamically allocated and holds formatted version information including normal firmware, recovery firmware, bootloader, and system resource versions.
- **Use**: Used to display the firmware version information in a text layer within a window.


# Data Structures

---
### VersionData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``app_data``: A pointer to `MfgFuncTestData`, which holds application-specific data.
    - ``label``: A `TextLayer` structure used to display text on the screen.
- **Description**: Holds data related to the version display window, including application-specific data and a text layer for displaying version information.


# Functions

---
### version\_window\_button\_up<!-- {{#callable:version_window_button_up}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_apps/mfg_func_test_version.h#L44>)

Handles the button-up event to manage window stack behavior based on click count.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the click recognizer that identifies the button event.
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object that receives the button-up event.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increment the static variable `s_click_count`.
    - Check if `s_click_count` is greater than 0.
    - If true, set `animated` to `false` and call `window_stack_pop(animated)` to remove the window from the stack without animation.
- **Output**: No direct output; modifies the window stack based on button-up events.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_stack_pop`](<../../applib/ui/window_stack.c.md#window_stack_pop>)


---
### version\_window\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:version_window_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_apps/mfg_func_test_version.h#L54>)

Configures click event handlers for all buttons in a window context.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``context``: A pointer to the context data that is passed to the click handler.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Iterates over all button IDs from `BUTTON_ID_BACK` to `NUM_BUTTONS`.
    - For each button ID, subscribes to raw click events using [`window_raw_click_subscribe`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_raw_click_subscribe>).
    - Sets the click handler to `version_window_button_up` for button-up events.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_raw_click_subscribe`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_raw_click_subscribe>)


---
### version\_get\_version\_str<!-- {{#callable:version_get_version_str}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_apps/mfg_func_test_version.h#L61>)

Formats and writes firmware and system version information into a buffer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``buf``: A character buffer where the formatted version string will be written.
    - ``size``: The size of the buffer `buf`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `FirmwareMetadata` structures for normal and recovery firmware.
    - Copy running firmware metadata into `normal_fw` and recovery firmware metadata into `recovery_fw`.
    - Retrieve the system resource version using `resource_get_system_version()`.
    - Read the bootloader version using `boot_version_read()`.
    - Check if `normal_fw.version_short` is a valid UTF-8 string; if not, set `normal_version` to "???".
    - Check if `recovery_fw.version_short` is a valid UTF-8 string; if not, set `recovery_version` to "???".
    - Format the version information into the buffer `buf` using [`sniprintf`](<../../../../tests/overrides/default/stdio.h.md#sniprintf>), including normal and recovery firmware versions, bootloader version, and system resource version.
- **Output**: A formatted string containing the normal and recovery firmware versions, bootloader version, and system resource version is written into the provided buffer `buf`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`resource_get_system_version`](<../../resource/resource.c.md#resource_get_system_version>)
    - [`utf8_is_valid_string`](<../../applib/graphics/utf8.c.md#utf8_is_valid_string>)
    - [`sniprintf`](<../../../../tests/overrides/default/stdio.h.md#sniprintf>)


---
### version\_window\_load<!-- {{#callable:version_window_load}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_apps/mfg_func_test_version.h#L91>)

Initializes and configures the version display window with version information and a text label.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object that is being loaded.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve user data associated with the `window` and store it in `data`.
    - Get the root layer of the `window` and store it in `root`.
    - Allocate memory for `s_version_str` to hold the version string.
    - Call [`version_get_version_str`](<#version_get_version_str>) to populate `s_version_str` with version information.
    - Initialize a `TextLayer` using `data->label` and set its properties such as position, background color, text color, text content, and font.
    - Add the `TextLayer` to the `root` layer as a child.
    - Initialize `s_click_count` to 0.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `window` and its associated data.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)
    - [`version_get_version_str`](<#version_get_version_str>)
    - [`text_layer_init`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)
    - [`text_layer_set_background_color`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_background_color>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text_color`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text_color>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)
    - [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>)


---
### version\_window\_unload<!-- {{#callable:version_window_unload}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_apps/mfg_func_test_version.h#L109>)

Releases memory allocated for the version string when the window is unloaded.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object that is being unloaded.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `free` to release the memory allocated for `s_version_str`.
    - Casts `window` to `void` to indicate that it is unused in the function.
- **Output**: No return value.

### push\_version\_window<!-- {{#callable:push_version_window}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_apps/mfg_func_test_version.h#L114>)

Initializes and displays a version window with specific configurations and handlers.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``app_data``: A pointer to `MfgFuncTestData` structure that contains application-specific data, including the `version_window` to be initialized and displayed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Declare a static `VersionData` structure `s_version_data` and assign `app_data` to its `app_data` field.
    - Get the `version_window` from `app_data`.
    - Initialize the `version_window` with the name "Mfg Func Test Version" using [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>).
    - Set the `version_window` to override the back button using [`window_set_overrides_back_button`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_overrides_back_button>).
    - Set the click configuration provider for `version_window` using `window_set_click_config_provider_with_context`, passing `version_window_click_config_provider` as the provider and `version_window` as the context.
    - Set the window handlers for `version_window` using [`window_set_window_handlers`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_window_handlers>), specifying `version_window_load` and `version_window_unload` for load and unload events respectively.
    - Set the user data for `version_window` to `s_version_data` using [`window_set_user_data`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>).
    - Set the `version_window` to fullscreen using [`window_set_fullscreen`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_fullscreen>).
    - Push the `version_window` onto the window stack without animation using `window_stack_push`.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it modifies the `version_window` within `app_data` and pushes it onto the window stack.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_overrides_back_button`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_overrides_back_button>)
    - [`window_set_window_handlers`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_window_handlers>)
    - [`window_set_user_data`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>)
    - [`window_set_fullscreen`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_fullscreen>)
